response_headers.X-Vqd-Hash-1: PRESENT (long hash string starting with KGFzeW5jIGZ1bmN0aW9u...)
response_headers.note: X-Vqd-Hash-1 header is present in response. x-vqd-4 was NOT observed as a separate response header.

results.request_headers_note: Request likely includes x-vqd-4 token in request headers (standard DuckDuckGo AI Chat auth). DevTools showed Referrer-Policy: origin in general section.

sse_format.event_type_field: All events use SSE 'event:' field with type 'message' (shown as 'm...' in DevTools)

event_sequence.description: Chat title assignment. Format: [CHAT_TITLE:<title>]. Not JSON.
Response start event. JSON with 'id' field prefixed with 'rs_'. May contain: id, created, model, object fields.
Message content event. JSON with 'id' field prefixed with 'msg_'. Contains the actual AI response text. Key fields likely: id, role, content, model, message (the actual response text '4').
Stream termination signal. Not JSON. Same pattern as OpenAI SSE.

template_implications.content_carrier_event: msg_ event (order 3) - contains the actual AI response text
template_implications.rs_event_purpose: Response metadata/start signal with unique response ID
template_implications.chat_title_event: Optional first event, assigns conversation title
template_implications.ping_event: Keep-alive, can be ignored by parser
template_implications.all_events_type_message: SSE event type is 'message' for all events
template_implications.current_template_mismatch: Current template uses {"message":"...","action":"success"} which does NOT match. Real format uses rs_/msg_ ID-prefixed JSON objects + control signals ([CHAT_TITLE:], [PING], [DONE]).

results.notes: Response tab showed 'Nothing to preview' for raw response data (common for SSE streams in Chrome DevTools). EventStream tab successfully showed all 5 events. X-Vqd-Hash-1 header present in response (authentication token for next request). CORS is wildcard (*), no credentials required. Simple SSE format similar to OpenAI pattern.
